hero_subline: Timed OSCE stations, desk tasks, practical checklists, and medical-emergency vivas — you interact, submit your work, and get structured feedback mapped to what you actually did.

experience_section.title: What you actually do
experience_section.lead: No stock photos or demo videos needed: every format mirrors how you work in the exam cubicle or at the desk. You type, choose, and converse — the platform scores your real inputs.

formats.label: Actor OSCE stations
formats.summary: Read the station brief, then chat with a simulated patient or relative under exam timing.
bullets: One-minute reading time, then five-minute active station (official-style timing)
Type your questions and explanations — the patient responds in character
Hidden clinical facts unlock only when you ask appropriately (like a real actor station)
Emotion and trust shift based on how you communicate

formats.label: Written desk stations
formats.summary: Unstaffed desk work: prescriptions, radiograph reports, HTM audit forms, treatment plans, and referral letters.
bullets: Fill structured forms and free-text fields — not multiple choice
Radiograph stations score against verification tables for findings you report
Prescription and HTM tasks check dose, safety, and documentation detail
Submit when ready; marking runs against the station rubric

formats.label: Practical study topics
formats.summary: Self-practice checklists for hands-on technique — examiner mark-sheet style, not a live manikin exam.
bullets: Work through step-by-step procedures at your own pace
Tick off checklist items as you would on an examiner sheet
Band your overall performance (exceeds / meets / below standard)
See which critical steps you missed before you sit the real OSCE

formats.label: Medical emergencies
formats.summary: Oral viva stems plus verbal BLS practice — procedural timing and examiner questions, not hands-on CPR coaching.
bullets: Answer viva questions aloud in your own words (typed submission)
Verbal BLS walkthrough with timing prompts aligned to examiner format
Themes cover anaphylaxis, asthma, hypoglycaemia, syncope, arrest, emergency drugs, and more
Structured review against canonical management steps

formats.label: 17-station circuit mock
formats.summary: Run a continuous mock exam session — actor, written, and practical slots sequenced with rest and pilot breaks that do not score.
bullets: Official-style circuit flow: active stations, rest slots, and pilot slots
Station-to-station timing like exam day
Build stamina and pacing before you pay for a physical mock course
Review each station after the circuit or return to weak areas in the library

feedback_section.title: Feedback based on your performance
feedback_section.lead: When you end a session, review is generated from what you submitted — transcript, forms, checklist ticks, or viva answers — not generic tips.

feedback_items.title: Pass / fail and domain scores
feedback_items.body: Overall outcome plus rubric domains (history, diagnosis, management, communication, professionalism) scored against station criteria.

feedback_items.title: Checklist guidance
feedback_items.body: Item-by-item breakdown of what you met, missed, or critically failed — linked to GDC-style marking language.

feedback_items.title: Strengths and gaps
feedback_items.body: Concrete strengths, improvements, and missed items drawn from your session, with evidence quotes from your transcript where relevant.

feedback_items.title: Common mistake callouts
feedback_items.body: Pitfalls catalogued for that station type — surfaced when your attempt matches a known weak pattern.

feedback_items.title: Official guidance links
feedback_items.body: ORE-aligned guidance excerpts and learning resources so you know what examiners expect next time.

feedback_items.title: Reattempt tracking
feedback_items.body: Standard plans include one completed attempt per station; Pro includes three so you can practise, review, and improve before your allowance runs out.

value_section.title: Why candidates pay for structured practice
value_section.lead: Physical mock circuits and revision courses cost hundreds of pounds for a single day. ORE-UK.ORG gives you a full library, exam-style interaction, and personalised feedback you can repeat on your schedule.

value_points.title: Real interaction, not passive revision
value_points.body: You are not watching videos or tapping flashcards — you run timed stations and submit work exactly as in the cubicle.

value_points.title: Hundreds of stations in one subscription
value_points.body: 304 actor scenarios plus written, practical, circuit, and ME content — far more variety than a one-day mock.

value_points.title: Feedback you can act on
value_points.body: Every attempt ends with a review page: scores, checklists, and next steps — so you know what to fix before exam day.

value_points.title: Six months of access
value_points.body: Paid packages include six months from purchase (when checkout launches) — spread practice across your revision timeline.

how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.lead: From registration to review in four steps — the same flow for OSCE stations, circuit mocks, and medical emergencies.

steps.title: Choose your focus
steps.body: Pick a single station, a written desk task, a practical topic, an ME theme, or a full circuit mock from the library.

steps.title: Run under exam conditions
steps.body: Reading time, timers, and station rules match official OSCE / ME format. Interact with the patient, complete forms, or answer viva stems.

steps.title: Submit and finish
steps.body: End the station when time is up or you have completed the task. Your inputs are saved for marking.

steps.title: Review and improve
steps.body: Open your feedback report: domains, checklists, critical fails, and guidance. Reattempt on Pro plans while you still have allowance.
